h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I'm using the term, but I still need to check the cell after the colon.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue applying the header-then-value rule to successive columns, which is exactly what each turn does.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates genuine pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (header before colon, value after) across all three turns without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; all tutor turns continue the prior lesson plan unchanged.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; all tutor turns continue the prior lesson plan unchanged.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates genuine pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (header before colon, value after) across all three turns without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates genuine pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (header before colon, value after) across all three turns without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The checklist feels tight, but it keeps me from jumping to "went up" yet.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue restating the axis-label splitting rule and fill in the blank point-check template, exactly as each prior turn has done.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same procedural prompt across T1, T2, and T3 without changing route, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible, and no later tutor turn adapts to a revised learner framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible, and no later tutor turn adapts to a revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same procedural prompt across T1, T2, and T3 without changing route, representation, or register.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same procedural prompt across T1, T2, and T3 without changing route, representation, or register.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The strip helps, but I need to check the label first or my hand will rush it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is steadily internalizing the tail-on-food, head-on-eater rule and each turn is expected to confirm incremental procedural mastery.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The strip is the rule: tail on the food, head on who gets energy. Grass to rabbit already matches it. On rabbit-hawk, name the food first, then set the pencil tail there.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T1 reorients from naming to physical object placement (pencil as embodied rule), a modest register shift in response to L1's chase-image interference, but the move is incremental rather than a decisive route change.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Because no pivot learner turn exists, there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to; T4 closes with a routine-consolidation move that was already implicit in the prior tutor turns.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Because no pivot learner turn exists, there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to; T4 closes with a routine-consolidation move that was already implicit in the prior tutor turns.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The strip is the rule: tail on the food, head on who gets energy. Grass to rabbit already matches it. On rabbit-hawk, name the food first, then set the pencil tail there.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T1 reorients from naming to physical object placement (pencil as embodied rule), a modest register shift in response to L1's chase-image interference, but the move is incremental rather than a decisive route change.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The strip is the rule: tail on the food, head on who gets energy. Grass to rabbit already matches it. On rabbit-hawk, name the food first, then set the pencil tail there.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: T1 reorients from naming to physical object placement (pencil as embodied rule), a modest register shift in response to L1's chase-image interference, but the move is incremental rather than a decisive route change.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue placing each successive force card at its correct contact and asking a clarifying directional question, following the tutor's sequential prompts.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same sequential card-placement routine throughout with no pressure from the learner triggering a change of route, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The bend keeps its tile; the separate printed line takes the next number.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue confirming that each contour carries one elevation number as the tutor adds more tiles.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Same printed line, same tile. Separate printed line, separate tile. Read one spot on each line.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a second tile and a contrastive comparison (160 vs 180), which is a modest representational addition, but it extends the same drill rather than breaking a failed tutoring habit in response to learner pressure.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can occur.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a second tile and a contrastive comparison (160 vs 180), which is a modest representational addition, but it extends the same drill rather than breaking a failed tutoring habit in response to learner pressure.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Same printed line, same tile. Separate printed line, separate tile. Read one spot on each line.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a second tile and a contrastive comparison (160 vs 180), which is a modest representational addition, but it extends the same drill rather than breaking a failed tutoring habit in response to learner pressure.
